name: Chicago Avenue Garage Federal Credit Union
description_short: Chicago Avenue Garage FCU is a small, member-owned credit union serving employees of Chicago Avenue Garage and their families with savings accounts and personal loans.
description_long:
Chicago Avenue Garage Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit financial cooperative located at 4909 W. Division Street in Chicago, Illinois. Founded to serve a specific employee group, it operates as a member-owned institution with an elected volunteer board and supervisory committee dedicated to ensuring safety and effectiveness. The credit union emphasizes personalized service, noting that it is "small enough to get to know each member personally, including their name, face and phone voice."

The institution offers a focused range of financial products including savings accounts insured up to $250,000 by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration), unsecured personal loans, secured loans at 12% APR, Christmas loans ($1,000-$2,000 for 10 months), and short-term emergency loans ($500 maximum for 6 months at 25% APR). Savings accounts feature competitive interest rates. Loan applications are processed through a download-and-email system rather than online origination.

What distinguishes Chicago Avenue Garage FCU is its highly specialized membership eligibility—only current employees of Chicago Avenue Garage and their immediate family members (spouses, children, siblings, in-laws, step-relations, and grandchildren) may join. This narrow field creates a close-knit member community but severely limits growth potential and accessibility. The credit union operates limited hours (Monday-Friday, 10:00 AM-2:30 PM, closed the last 2 working days of each month), reflecting its small-scale operation.

While Chicago Avenue Garage FCU offers the fundamental benefits of credit union membership—non-profit structure, member ownership, and NCUA insurance—it is extremely limited in scope. It serves only a niche employee group and their families, making it inaccessible to the general public. The emergency loan product at 25% APR is notably expensive for short-term borrowing, though still below payday loan rates. The limited operating hours and manual application process may inconvenience members accustomed to digital banking. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: NCUA-insured savings accounts up to $250,000 per member
Not-for-profit structure with volunteer board dedicated to member welfare
Personalized service with staff knowing members by name and relationship
Secured loans available at 12% APR for qualified borrowers
Christmas loans specifically designed for holiday expenses
Member-owned cooperative structure with democratic governance
cons: Membership restricted only to Chicago Avenue Garage employees and their immediate family—not open to general public
Emergency loans capped at $500 with 25% APR, which is expensive despite being below payday loan rates
Very limited operating hours (10 AM-2:30 PM weekdays only, closed last 2 working days of month)
Manual loan application process requiring download, completion, and email submission rather than online origination
Extremely small institution with minimal digital banking presence or modern conveniences
best_for: Current and former employees of Chicago Avenue Garage seeking safe savings with NCUA insurance
Immediate family members of Chicago Avenue Garage employees needing personal or seasonal loans
Members prioritizing personal relationships with credit union staff over digital convenience
Borrowers needing Christmas or short-term emergency loans with fixed terms
diagnosis: Chicago Avenue Garage FCU is best for current employees of Chicago Avenue Garage and their eligible family members seeking a safe savings account with NCUA insurance and straightforward personal lending. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—this credit union is completely inaccessible to the general public and serves only a specific employee group, making it unsuitable for most consumers seeking a credit union alternative to traditional banking.
services: Savings accounts with NCUA insurance up to $250,000
Short-term emergency loans ($500 maximum, 6-month terms, 25% APR)
Membership for eligible Chicago Avenue Garage employees
Membership for immediate family of current members
Loan application processing via downloadable forms
Member financial counseling and decision support
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 4909 W Division St Ste 403, Chicago, IL 60651
